- We need self-contained documentation for architecture, usage, and limitations.

Scope
- Document parser, init directives, IR, layout pipeline, routing, scaling tiers, renderer.
- Document supported Mermaid syntax + style mapping + compatibility matrix.
- Document config flags, capability profiles, guard/degradation behavior, and hyperlink policy.
- Include JSONL log schema and debug overlay usage.
- Provide examples + troubleshooting for width issues.

Considerations
- Focus on determinism guarantees and terminal constraints.
- Include roadmap for diagram types and unsupported features.

Success Criteria
- Docs sufficient to extend engine without external notes.
- Users can understand how scaling tiers affect  {"id":"bd-10ck","title":"Validation Pipeline: Expected-Cost Ordering (Bayes + Early Exit)","description":"## Goal
Minimize validation latency by ordering validators by expected cost and failure probability, while keeping deterministic behavior.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Model each validator i with cost c_i and failure probability p_i (posterior). Expected cost of an ordering pi is:
E[cost] = sum_k (c_{pi_k} * prod_{j<k} (1 - p_{pi_j}))
Choose ordering that minimizes expected cost. Update p_i online from observed failures with Beta priors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic ordering under fixed seed + history.
- Latency improvement vs static ordering (documented).
- Explainable ranking with evidence ledger of p_i and c_i.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_expected_cost_formula: ordering matches brute-force for small n.
- unit_posterior_update: p_i updates with success/failure.
- perf_validation_latency: measure p50/p95 before/after; log.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay validation logs; verify ordering adjustments and deterministic output.
- Log: c_i, p_i, ordering, total latency.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:50.749651642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:52:05.141886791Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:52:05.141858537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-10i","title":"FrankenTUI: Complete Implementation Epic","description":"# FrankenTUI (ftui) - Master Epic

## Vision
FrankenTUI is a deliberately minimal, high-performance terminal UI kernel that fuses the best surviving abstractions from three Rust codebases (rich_rust, charmed_rust, opentui_rust) into one coherent system.

## Core Promise
ftui is designed so you can build a Claude Code / Codex-class agent harness UI without flicker, without cursor corruption, and without sacrificing native scrollback.

## Three-Library Synthesis (conceptual)
- opentui_rust: Cell grid, diff algorithm, alpha blending, scissor/opacity stacks, grapheme pool (cache-optimal 16-byte cells)
- rich_rust: Segment abstraction, markup parser, measurement protocol (Cow<str> for zero-copy)
- charmed_rust: LipGloss styling, CSS-like properties, theme system, Elm architecture (bitflags property tracking)

## Non-Negotiables (Engineering Contract)
1. One writer owns the terminal - serialized output
2. Untrusted bytes never executed - sanitize by default
3. Diffed + buffered UI only - no ad-hoc println
4. Inline-first is real - preserve scrollback
5. Safe by default, unsafe isolated (repo policy: unsafe forbidden)
6. Deterministic by design
7. Correctness continuously verified

## Performance Budgets (v1)
- Present (80x24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
- Cell comparison: < 1ns
- 16 bytes per cell (4 per cache line)

## Success Criteria (v1 = Done)
(Directly derived from plan Section 0.11.)

- [ ] Inline mode default is stable (streaming logs + UI does not corrupt scrollback/cursor).
- [ ] PTY tests confirm cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- [ ] Presenter/diff correctness validated by terminal-model tests.
- [ ] Unicode width correctness proven by corpus tests (ZWJ/emoji/combining).
- [ ] Style system is deterministic and documented (explicit masks + inheritance).
- [ ] Runtime supports update/view loop + ticks + batch/sequence cmds + deterministic simulator.
- [ ] Harness-essential widgets exist and are tested (viewport/log viewer, status/panel, prompt/text input, progress/spinner).
- [ ] Docs exist: agent harness tutorial, inline vs alt-screen trade-offs, one-writer rule guidance, windows limitations, compatibility matrix.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:04:22.713361602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:05:30.098857536Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:05:30.098791652Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3ufh","title":"Tick command delivers model updates","description":"Problem: Cmd::Tick currently only triggers redraws. Model::update never sees a tick message, so periodic logic cannot run without external input and tick cadence is unclear.

Scope:
- Define explicit tick semantics (Event::Tick or equivalent) that flows through the same update pipeline as input events.
- Schedule ticks using monotonic Instant; integrate with poll timeout and resize debounce without busy-spin or missed ticks.
- Ensure ticks are disabled cleanly (Duration::ZERO or a dedicated no-tick command) and do not starve input events.
- Document tick behavior and ordering guarantees in runtime docs or comments.

Testing:
- Unit tests in ftui-runtime: tick cadence, coalescing, disable or stop behavior, and no starvation with resize debounce.
- Unit tests covering event ordering when input and tick occur near-simultaneously.
- E2E harness test that logs N ticks, exits deterministically, and produces JSON summary plus PTY capture.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Model receives tick updates reliably and deterministically.
- No busy loop when ticks are disabled or when waiting on input.
- Tests prove cadence, ordering, and disable semantics; docs updated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"TurquoiseCliff","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:26.967603358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:48:55.837816010Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:48:55.837795221Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Added two tests: (1) stride=0 parity with stride=1 to verify stride normalization path, and (2) non-even x upscale nearest-neighbor mapping for 3->5 columns (expected source columns [0,0,1,1,2]). Existing half-block fg/bg and stride behavior tests remain in place.  {"id":"bd-3vbf","title":"Visual Polish Epic: Transform Demo Showcase into Jaw-Dropping Experience","description":"## Epic Overview

Transform the FrankenTUI demo-showcase from 'functional demonstration' to 'jaw-droppingly impressive work of art' - every section should be a visual masterpiece that showcases what's possible in terminal UI.

## Inspiration Sources

1. **beads_viewer** (/dp/beads_viewer) - Famous for looking amazing
   - Sophisticated semantic color system with status/priority/type badges
   - Pre-computed styles for performance and consistency
   - Micro-visualizations (sparklines, mini-bars, heatmaps)
   - Rich iconography with emoji/symbols
   - Multi-view modes with smooth transitions
   - WCAG AA compliant contrast ratios

2. **charmed_rust** (/dp/charmed_rust) - Already-ported Charmbracelet components
   - lipgloss: CSS-like declarative styling
   - bubbles: Rich UI components (spinner, textinput, progress, etc.)
   - harmonica: Spring physics for smooth animations
   - glamour: Markdown rendering with syntax highlighting

## Goals

1. **Design System Foundation** - Semantic colors, spacing tokens, pre-computed styles
2. **Chrome Polish** - Tab bar, status bar, help overlay improvements
3. **Screen-by-Screen Excellence** - Each demo screen is a work of art
4. **New Capabilities** - Add missing features that beads_viewer/charmed_rust have
5. **Micro-interactions** - Subtle animations, smooth transitions, polish
6. **Accessibility** - WCAG AA compliance, graceful degradation

## Success Criteria

- Visitors say 'wow' when they see the demo
- Each screen could be a standalone showcase
- Colors convey meaning, not just decoration
- Animations feel natural and purposeful
- Performance remains excellent

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Color: operate in perceptual space (OKLCH/CIELAB) with hard contrast constraints; precompute palettes via constrained optimization and prove WCAG bounds in tests.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child beads specify unit tests and E2E scripts with structured JSONL logging.
- [ ] Visual polish changes are validated via snapshot updates and explicit acceptance screenshots.
- [ ] Performance budgets documented for any animation or rendering change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T21:40:47.240759059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:00:09.526441879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:00:09.526412734Z","close_reason":"All subtasks closed, visual polish implemented across all screens","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","showcase"]}
 {"id":"bd-3vbf.1","title":"Design System: Semantic Color Palette with Status/Priority/Type Colors","description":"## Background

The beads_viewer achieves its polished look through a sophisticated color system where **every color conveys meaning**:
- Status colors: OPEN (green), IN_PROGRESS (blue), BLOCKED (red), CLOSED (muted)
- Priority colors: P0/CRITICAL (hot red), P1/HIGH (orange), P2/MEDIUM (yellow), P3/LOW (cyan)
- Type colors: Bug (red), Feature (purple), Task (blue), Epic (gold)

Each semantic color has:
- Primary foreground color
- Background tint for badges/highlights
- Contrast-validated text color for backgrounds
- Pre-computed Style objects for performance

Currently FrankenTUI's theme.rs has basic colors but lacks semantic meaning.

## Implementation

1. Add semantic color modules to theme.rs:
```rust
pub mod status {
    pub const OPEN: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(80, 220, 140);
    pub const OPEN_BG: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgba(80, 220, 140, 40);
    pub const IN_PROGRESS: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(100, 180, 255);
    pub const BLOCKED: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(255, 100, 100);
    pub const CLOSED: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(120, 120, 140);
}

pub mod priority {
    pub const CRITICAL: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(255, 50, 50);
    pub const HIGH: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(255, 150, 50);
    pub const MEDIUM: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(255, 220, 80);
    pub const LOW: PackedRgba = PackedRgba::rgb(100, 200, 255);
}
```

2. Create SemanticStyles struct with pre-computed styles
3. Add contrast validation utility
4. Document color meanings

## Justification

Colors are the foundation of visual design. Semantic colors:
- Reduce cognitive load (users learn color meanings)
- Create visual hierarchy
- Enable instant recognition of status/priority
- Make the UI feel professional and intentional

## Files to Modify

- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/theme.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Status colors defined (open/progress/blocked/closed)
- [ ] Priority colors defined (P0-P4)
- [ ] Type colors defined (error/warning/info/success)
- [ ] Background tint variants for each
- [ ] Contrast ratios >= 4.5:1 (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Pre-computed SemanticStyles available","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T21:41:05.364954759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:04.625588117Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:04.625568560Z","close_reason":"Implemented semantic palette + contrast utilities + SemanticStyles","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["polish","showcase","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vbf.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":446,"issue_id":"bd-3vbf.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests Required

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod semantic_color_tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn status_colors_have_valid_contrast() {
        // WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 contrast ratio
        fn contrast_ratio(fg: PackedRgba, bg: PackedRgba) -> f64 {
            let lum_fg = relative_luminance(fg);
            let lum_bg = relative_luminance(bg);
            let lighter = lum_fg.max(lum_bg);
            let darker = lum_fg.min(lum_bg);
            (lighter + 0.05) / (darker + 0.05)
        }
        
        fn relative_luminance(c: PackedRgba) -> f64 {
            let r = srgb_to_linear(c.r() as f64 / 255.0);
            let g = srgb_to_linear(c.g() as f64 / 255.0);
            let b = srgb_to_linear(c.b() as f64 / 255.0);
            0.2126 * r + 0.7152 * g + 0.0722 * b
        }
        
        fn srgb_to_linear(c: f64) -> f64 {
            if c <= 0.03928 { c / 12.92 } else { ((c + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4) }
        }
        
        assert!(contrast_ratio(status::OPEN, bg::BASE) >= 4.5);
        assert!(contrast_ratio(status::BLOCKED, bg::BASE) >= 4.5);
        assert!(contrast_ratio(status::PROGRESS, bg::BASE) >= 4.5);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn all_priority_colors_distinct() {
        let colors = [priority::CRITICAL, priority::HIGH, priority::MEDIUM, priority::LOW];
        for (i, c1) in colors.iter().enumerate() {
            for (j, c2) in colors.iter().enumerate() {
                if i != j {
                    assert_ne!(c1, c2);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn status_bg_variants_have_low_opacity() {
        // Background variants should have alpha < 128 for tinting
        assert!(status::OPEN_BG.a() < 128);
        assert!(status::BLOCKED_BG.a() < 128);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3vbf.10","title":"Data Viz: Add Sparklines Widget for Trend Visualization","description":"## Background

beads_viewer uses sparklines extensively for showing trends:
- 8-level Unicode block characters: ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█
- Auto-scaling with min/max detection
- Color gradients from cold to hot
- Compact visualization in tight spaces

Sparklines are essential for data-rich dashboards.

## Current State

charts.rs has chart implementations but sparklines may not be fully featured.
charmed_rust has sparkline support that could be referenced.

## Implementation

1. Sparkline widget in ftui-widgets or ftui-extras:
```rust
pub struct Sparkline<'a> {
    data: &'a [f64],
    min: Option<f64>,
    max: Option<f64>,
    color: PackedRgba,
    gradient: Option<(PackedRgba, PackedRgba)>,
}

impl Sparkline<'_> {
    const CHARS: &'static [char] = &[' ', '▁', '▂', '▃', '▄', '▅', '▆', '▇', '█'];
    
    pub fn render_string(&self) -> String {
        let min = self.min.unwrap_or_else(|| self.data.iter().copied().fold(f64::INFINITY, f64::min));
        let max = self.max.unwrap_or_else(|| self.data.iter().copied().fold(f64::NEG_INFINITY, f64::max));
        let range = max - min;
        
        self.data.iter().map(|&v| {
            let normalized = if range > 0.0 { (v - min) / range } else { 0.5 };
            let idx = (normalized * 8.0).round() as usize;
            Self::CHARS[idx.min(8)]
        }).collect()
    }
}
```

2. Add to Data Viz screen with sample data

3. Use in Dashboard screen for system metrics

## Files to Modify

- crates/ftui-extras/src/charts.rs or new sparkline.rs
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/data_viz.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sparkline widget implemented
- [ ] Auto-scaling works correctly
- [ ] Color gradient support
- [ ] Demo showcases sparklines
- [ ] Used in at least 2 screens

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Color: operate in perceptual space (OKLCH/CIELAB) with hard contrast constraints; precompute palettes via constrained optimization and prove WCAG bounds in tests.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

Added enhanced ftui_widgets::Sparkline with degradation support, better edge case handling, and 19 tests as a bonus.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dataviz","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vbf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vbf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":447,"issue_id":"bd-3vbf.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests Required

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod sparkline_tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn sparkline_normalizes_correctly() {
        let data = vec![0.0, 50.0, 100.0];
        let spark = Sparkline::new(&data);
        let rendered = spark.render_string();
        assert_eq!(rendered.chars().count(), 3);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn sparkline_handles_empty_data() {
        let data: Vec<f64> = vec![];
        let spark = Sparkline::new(&data);
        assert_eq!(spark.render_string(), "");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn sparkline_handles_constant_data() {
        let data = vec![50.0, 50.0, 50.0];
        let spark = Sparkline::new(&data);
        assert_eq!(spark.render_string().chars().count(), 3);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn sparkline_respects_explicit_min_max() {
        let data = vec![25.0, 50.0, 75.0];
        let spark = Sparkline::new(&data).min(0.0).max(100.0);
        assert_eq!(spark.render_string().chars().count(), 3);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-pt0dv","title":"Mermaid Parser Core (Tokenizer + AST)","description":"Background\n- We must parse Mermaid fenced blocks into a deterministic AST.\n- Mermaid syntax varies by diagram type; a shared tokenizer + AST core prevents drift.\n\nScope\n- Implement tokenizer: keywords, identifiers, arrows, brackets, directives, strings, comments.\n- Parse Mermaid init directives (%%{init: {...}}%%) into AST nodes.\n- Parse comments/annotations and ignore or retain for diagnostics.\n- Build AST for all supported diagram types (flowchart, sequence, state, gantt, class, ER, mindmap, pie).\n- Parse subgraph/cluster blocks and diagram direction directives.\n- Parse styling directives (classDef/class/style/linkStyle) as AST nodes.\n- Parse link/click directives into metadata (even if degraded at render).\n- Robust error reporting with source spans (line/col) and expected tokens.\n- Error recovery: resynchronize at line boundaries to continue AST build when possible.\n- Deterministic normalization of whitespace + line endings.\n\nConsiderations\n- Avoid parser generators unless already used; keep dependencies minimal.\n- Avoid allocations in hot loops (use slices/indices).\n- Parser must never panic on malformed input.\n\nTests / Validation\n- Unit tests for lexing + parsing per diagram type.\n- Golden AST fixtures for canonical samples.\n- Error-case tests asserting span + error kind.\n- Seeded fuzz tests for tokenizer + parser to ensure no panics.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- AST captures nodes, edges, labels, directives, and diagram type.\n- Parser errors are structured and location-aware; recovery yields partial AST when possible.\n- Parser returns identical AST for semantically equivalent whitespace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyCompass","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:38:02.271400086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069074956Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069053856Z","close_reason":"Mermaid core parser: directives/comments/subgraph/style/link + error recovery + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ptyoh","title":"Fix demo showcase mouse navigation + hit regions","description":"Purpose:
- Restore intuitive mouse interactions across the demo showcase, especially menu section clicks that used to work.

Background:
- User reports menu section clicks no longer navigate; mouse should do the obvious across the entire demo showcase.

Scope:
- Audit hit‑region registration for menu items and screen lists.
- Verify mouse event routing (click, hover, scroll) in showcase runtime and ensure it maps to the correct HitId.
- Fix regression so left‑click selects/activates the intended section/screen.
- Ensure hover highlights and click activation align with current layout/scroll offsets.

Testing:
- Add unit tests for hit‑region registration mapping to menu items.
- Add E2E test that simulates mouse clicks on menu sections and verifies navigation.
- Add JSONL logging for mouse events: event type, x/y, hit id, resolved action.

Acceptance:
- Clicking any menu section navigates reliably.
- Mouse interactions feel intuitive across the demo showcase.
- Tests + logs prevent regression.

 {"id":"bd-qsbe.1","title":"Setup: Create ftui-demo-showcase binary crate and Cargo.toml","description":"Create the new binary crate at crates/ftui-demo-showcase/. STEPS: (1) Create directory structure: crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/main.rs, crates/ftui-demo-showcase/Cargo.toml, crates/ftui-demo-showcase/data/ (for embedded assets). (2) Add to workspace: Edit root Cargo.toml to add 'crates/ftui-demo-showcase' to workspace members list. (3) Configure Cargo.toml dependencies: ftui-core, ftui-render, ftui-style, ftui-text (with features=['markup','normalization']), ftui-layout, ftui-runtime, ftui-widgets, ftui-extras (with features=['canvas','charts','clipboard','console','export','filesize','filepicker','forms','image','live','logging','markdown','stopwatch','syntax','timer','traceback']). Also add: serde_json (for JSON test data). (4) Create minimal src/main.rs that compiles: a trivial Model with empty view that quits on any key. (5) VERIFY: cargo check -p ftui-demo-showcase passes. cargo clippy -p ftui-demo-showcase -- -D warnings passes. cargo fmt -p ftui-demo-showcase -- --check passes. NOTE: Do NOT add pty-capture feature to extras (it requires portable-pty which is a dev-dep). The binary should be named ftui-demo-showcase. Edition = 2024 matching the workspace.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:42:24.730400120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:55:09.170914119Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:52:33.117398633Z","close_reason":"Created crates/ftui-demo-showcase/ with Cargo.toml (all ftui deps + all extras features + serde_json), src/main.rs (minimal skeleton with Model impl, tab bar, content area, help overlay, status bar), added to workspace. cargo check passes cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","phase0","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qsbe.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qsbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

SECTION B - Text Styles: Grid of 15+ cells showing every StyleFlags variant and combos: Bold, Dim, Italic, Underline, DoubleUnderline, CurlyUnderline, Blink, Reverse, Hidden, Strikethrough, Bold+Italic, Bold+Underline, Dim+Italic, All-flags. SECTION D - Interactive: TextInput with placeholder and mask. Paragraph with scroll. List with 10 items and selection. Table 5x4 with header and highlight. Tree 3 levels deep with expand/collapse. SECTION E - Data: ProgressBar at 0/25/50/75/100% (5 bars). Spinner DOTS and LINE variants. JsonView with sample nested JSON. LogRing with 20 entries showing circular buffer. SECTION F - Layout: Flex demo (H and V), Columns 3-col, Padding demo (visible padding), Group demo, Align 3x3 grid. SECTION G - Utility: Rule horizontal and vertical. Paginator (3 pages). Scrollbar H and V. ErrorBoundary catching intentional panic. CachedWidget with cache hit indicator. LayoutDebugger inline. WIDGETS EXERCISED (COMPLETE LIST): Block, Panel, Paragraph, TextInput, TextArea, List, Table, Tree, ProgressBar, Spinner, JsonView, LogViewer, LogRing, VirtualizedList, Scrollbar, Help, Rule, StatusLine, Paginator, ErrorBoundary, CachedWidget, DebugOverlay, ConstraintOverlay, LayoutDebugger, Columns, Group, Padding, Align. UNIT TESTS: (1) gallery_all_borders - verify 6 border types render. (2) gallery_all_styles - verify all StyleFlags produce distinct output. (3) gallery_color_gradient - verify TrueColor gradient.
